source: Sample mapping for NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 Moderate baseline ↔ SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria 2017. Hand-curated; replace with the AICPA-maintained crosswalk for production use.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Software, Infrastructure, and Architectures
mappings.notes: AC-2 covers user account lifecycle management; CC6.1 addresses logical access controls broadly.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — User Authentication
mappings.notes: AC-2 user provisioning/deprovisioning supports CC6.2 registration and authorization requirements.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Role-Based Access
mappings.notes: AC-3 enforces approved authorizations; CC6.3 grants access based on roles and least privilege.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Role-Based Access
mappings.notes: Both controls require enforcing least privilege via role-based access.
AU-2 establishes the events to log that feed CC7.2 anomaly monitoring.

mappings.source_control_title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
mappings.notes: AU-6 review/analysis of audit records is the analytical step in CC7.2 monitoring.
CM-2 baseline configurations are the starting point for CC8.1 change management.
CM-6 configuration settings drift detection feeds CC7.1 vulnerability detection.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — User Authentication
mappings.notes: IA-2 and CC6.2 both require uniquely identifying and authenticating users.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Software, Infrastructure, and Architectures
mappings.notes: IA-5 authenticator content protections support CC6.1 logical access security.
IR-4 incident handling and CC7.3 incident evaluation cover the same lifecycle.
Both controls require ongoing vulnerability detection and reporting.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — External User Authentication
mappings.notes: SC-7 boundary protection enforces external access controls in CC6.6.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Restriction and Disposal of Information
mappings.notes: SC-13 cryptographic protection secures data in transit covered by CC6.7.

mappings.target_control_title: Logical and Physical Access Controls — Restriction and Disposal of Information
mappings.notes: SC-28 encryption at rest protects information that CC6.7 governs movement and disposal of.
SI-2 flaw remediation is the response activity for vulnerabilities detected per CC7.1.
SI-4 and CC7.2 both require continuous monitoring for security-relevant events.
